title: The Spring, My Dear
content:
The spring, my dear,
    Is no longer spring.
    Does the blackbird sing
    What he sang last year?
    Are the skies the old
    Immemorial blue?
    Or am I, or are you,
    Grown cold?

    Though life be change,
    It is hard to bear
    When the old sweet air
    Sounds forced and strange.
    To be out of tune,
    Plain You and I . . .
    It were better to die,
    And soon!
meaning:
William Ernest Henley's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Spring, My Dear"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
